c: output diameter 52mm

t: ✨ Celebrating years of dedication at DOGA ✨
c:
Last week, we came together to honor the commitment and career journeys of our colleagues who have been part of the DOGA team for 10, 20, and 25 years.

It was a meaningful event where the traditional service pins were awarded to our colleagues who reached these important milestones — a tribute made even more special by the presence of loved ones and the warm support of coworkers.

The celebration continued with an outdoor gathering in the restaurant gardens, where laughter, stories, and great food created the perfect atmosphere to reflect on the past and look forward to the future.

To everyone who made this event possible: thank you.
Let’s keep building the future together!

t: Light Mobility Cluster and CIAC welcomed to DOGA
c:
On June 10, DOGA welcomed members of the Light Mobility Cluster and CIAC - Clúster de la Indústria d'Automoció de Catalunya for an #intercluster conference focused on #mobility: "From Motorcycles to Automotive – A Path to Industrial Competitiveness".

The event explored the main #challenges and emerging #opportunities in the components industry, highlighting the sector’s current #transformation.

Organized by both clusters, the conference featured insightful presentations from Eurecat - Centro Tecnológico and SGS, followed by a roundtable discussion with SHAD, Meleghy Automotive, and DOGA.

t: EUROBIKE has come to an end
c:
We is proud of the strong interest and positive response to our new e-bike motor, D-GO Mobility.
Developed by our #Electromobility Division, D-GO represents a significant step forward in our commitment to #smarter mobility #solutions and a greener future.

A Day of Speed, Team Spirit, and Fun!

Last week, our Technical Center turned into a racing arena for the DOGA GRAND PRIX — a unique internal event where employees competed on professional racing simulators, shared laughs, and enjoyed great food and music.

From thrilling final races to a generational showdown on the track, the day was filled with energy and connection. Events like this strengthen our culture and remind us how powerful teamwork can be — on and off the track.

Thanks to everyone who made it possible. See you at the next lap! 🏎️🎉

At DOGA, we are specialists in the design and manufacturing of 💧 Windshield Washer Tanks 💧 for agricultural machinery such as #tractors and #harvesters. Produced through high-precision blow molding, our plastic tanks — ranging from 1 to 40 liters, #standard or fully #customized — ensure durable and reliable performance under demanding conditions.
Agricultural machinery requires robust and efficient components: our washer #tanks are a guarantee of optimal operation, ensuring effective cleaning of vision systems and supporting the overall performance of the equipment.
Combined with our sealed #pumps (IP67) and customizable connectors, we offer a complete and secure #solution for every application.

🚜 Work better. See clearer. Enjoy the colours of the field.

DOGA GROUP CODE OF CONDUCT
Introduction
The companies which are part of DOGA (hereinafter referred to as DOGA) have, and have had since its foundation in 1958, an excellent reputation all over the world for corporate honesty. Integrity as a basis for business management, compliance with rules and laws, as well as our corporate values and commitment to sustainable development have been, are and will be the guidelines to be followed in decision-making processes and action taking.
Scope of the Code of Conduct
DOGA’s Code of Conduct has been laid down to formulate the principles by which the Group establishes its relations with employees, suppliers, partners and other participating parties. It is applicable to all the members of the organisation, from the Board of Directors to every single employee.
DOGA encourages its suppliers, customers and other related parties to adhere to these principles. The principles in this document will be applied to the evaluation of present and potential suppliers.
Business Principles
Compliance with current legislation
DOGA complies with the local rules and legislation in all the countries where it is present. In situations in which there is no specific legislation, DOGA applies its own rules based on its corporate values and corporate culture. If the law and the principles established in this code come into conflict, the law will prevail.
Relations with customers and suppliers
The transactions between DOGA and its customers and suppliers are characterized by fairness. DOGA will on no account offer any rewards or any advantage to those customers, potential customers or any of its representatives for breaking the applicable law or challenging the reasonable, ethical and generally accepted business practices.
DOGA employees should under no circumstances accept payments, presents or any other type of reward by a third party which could affect or appear to affect their objectivity in their decision making.
Financial information
All DOGA financial transactions must be reported according to the generally accepted accounting practices. The accounting records must show the nature of all transactions correctly and should never lead to error or deceit. DOGA will provide open, truthful, relevant and comprehensible information on time to those who require such information, if DOGA is under obligation to do so.
Conflicts of interest
DOGA employees and the members of the Board of Directors will develop their private and other external activities, such as their own financial interests, in a way in which they do not come into conflict, or seem to do so, with the interests of DOGA. Should any conflict of interest arise, it must be immediately reported to the Human Resources department by the person subject to the conflict.
Political involvement
DOGA observes neutrality with respect to political parties. Neither DOGA names nor DOGA assets should ever be used to promote the interests of political parties or any of their candidates.
Environmental Principles
Resources efficacy
DOGA designs its products and processes in such a way that the energy and raw materials are used efficiently and the waste and residual products are reduced to the minimum during the life cycle of products.
Preventive Principle
DOGA supports the preventive principle by avoiding materials and processes which could be environmentally risky and could put health at risk whenever alternative procedures are available.
Environmental performance
DOGA audits periodically its own plants and its suppliers’ plants, keeps a record of and reports their environmental action, emphasising the evaluation of potential risks for the products.
Human Rights and Work Practices
Human Rights
Within its scope of influence, DOGA supports and respects the protection of the human rights internationally proclaimed.
Non discrimination
DOGA employs and deals with its employees, customers, suppliers and other participating parties following a policy of no gender, race, religion, age, disability, sexual orientation, nationality, political opinion, affiliations, social or ethnic origin discrimination. Diversity is promoted at all levels.
Labour
No form of hard labour, child or compulsory labour will be tolerated. No one will be employed unless they have reached the age required to finish the compulsory secondary school. Freedom of association, the right to collective negotiation and the right to agreements will be protected in all DOGA workplaces.
Working atmosphere
All DOGA employees will be guaranteed the optimal conditions for a healthy and safe working atmosphere.
The responsibilities of Managers and Employees
It is DOGA Managers’ responsibility to communicate and inform about both the content and the spirit of this document within the organisation, and encourage the employees to report any behaviour which violates the principles established in this document. The explicit or implicit acceptance of questionable actions will not be tolerated.
Reports on no compliance with this code can be sent anonymously and confidentially to DOGA General Management. A communication channel is established through the e-mail address dpd@doga.es, which is received by the HR Director and the data protection delegate. Those who communicate violations in good faith, will not be subjected to punishment.
The application of this code will be accurate and coherent. The no compliance with its statements can lead to disciplinary actions.
Adherence to the United Nations Global Compact
DOGA joined the United Nations Global Compact on March 10th, 2015, therefore voluntarily acquiring a commitment to comply with the 10 Principles that are established in said Global Compact and taking up the obligation to prepare and share annual progress reports through the Global Compact web platform (www.pactomundial.org).
The Code of Conduct has been adopted by the Board of Directors of DOGA on June 27, 2007; first revision on March 24, 2015 on the occasion of the company's adherence to the UN Global Compact; second revision on April 1, 2019 on the occasion of the incorporation of the communication channel with Management; third revision on March 1, 2023 on the occasion of the acquisition of the DOGA companies by the new owners.
Any change will be published promptly.

DOGA S.A. - Autovía A-2, km. 583 - 08630 ABRERA - BARCELONA (SPAIN) · Tel. +34 93 770 46 00 · e-mail: doga@doga.es · www.doga.esGeneral Conditions of Sale – DOGA GROUPThe following General Conditions of Sale apply to DOGA GROUP, which includes the following subsidiaries: DOGA, SA - DOGA GESTIÓ, SLU – DOGA PARTS, SL. - DOGA Italia, S.R.L. - DOGA do Brasil, Ltda. - DOGA MEXICO COMPONENTES, SA DE C.V. - DOGA USA, Corp. - DOGA India, Pvt Ltd - DOGA NanTong Auto Parts Co., Ltd.1. Services ProvidedThe products/services will be supplied according to what has been established in the corresponding offer.When the offer is accepted, the client shall send an order to the DOGA GROUP subsidiary (“the subsidiary”), including the client’s order reference (if required), the offer reference, and the accepted points.2. OrdersThe contractual relationship starts when the client signs the first order; all orders are subject to acceptance by the subsidiary.All orders must be confirmed within 10 working days upon receipt. Claims cannot be made against unconfirmed orders.Dates and delivery times in the offer are indicative; actual delivery depends on supply times, manufacturing, and receipt of the order, and will be confirmed upon receipt.DOGA GROUP may unilaterally cancel manufacturing and/or delivery if insolvency arises or if no insurance has been taken out with the client or its subcontractors to cover business risks.3. Prices and InvoicesApplicable taxes are excluded from the offer; they will be included in the invoice.For regularly contracted services, the subsidiary may change prices for the following period.Advance payments are accepted up to 6 months before service. No price variation during this period, except 3.J). If canceled, the portion not supplied will be returned, minus 15% (admin/planning) and 100% of incurred material costs (including molds, if any).Acceptance implies payment under the offer’s specific terms, unaffected by any financial assistance or benefits.Prices may be reviewed if costs rise by more than 3%.Prices are exclusive and valid only for the company and location specified. Deliveries to other entities/locations may change prices.Prices are valid for the quantities in the offer. New quantities may change prices, or orders without quantity info may be rejected.No prompt payment discounts unless specifically stated in the contract.Deliveries and new orders will be automatically rejected if there are payment delays.Prices are set in the production site’s currency. If quoted in another currency, they are set to the exchange rate on the estimate day; if the rate varies by more than 5%, prices may be reviewed.Invoices are payable within 30 days of issue.Late payment accrues interest from the due date and collection expenses. If no rate agreed, interest is 8 percentage points above the ECB main refinancing rate.4. Claims / RefundsNo claims for losses caused by delivery date failures or quality issues unless specifically agreed.Quality-related claims will be managed under DOGA GROUP’s quality procedures.No refunds/payment requests without a positive resolution by DOGA’s Product Quality Department.A product is approved if accompanied by a client-completed Sample Acceptance Sheet or if the client sends a formal purchase order (not a prototype).DOGA GROUP is not liable for stoppages in the client’s production line.No quality claims accepted more than 60 days after client receipt.No returns accepted without an RMA issued by DOGA’s Product Quality Department.No returns/replacements until defective products are analyzed and DOGA GROUP responsibility is confirmed.If repair can be done by the client, DOGA GROUP may accept costs if informed in advance of labor rates and required hours.If DOGA GROUP opposes client repair, it may send its own staff; no unapproved costs will be borne.If an unauthorized product requires reprocessing at the client’s premises, DOGA GROUP will not bear costs unless specifically authorized or if presentation of goods was refused.No consequential losses accepted (e.g., reprocessing costs by the client).No design claims for products/projects not validated by the client. Sample acceptance implies approval and supply commitment. Claims for defects detected in accepted validation samples are not accepted unless a conditional acceptance (limited in time/quantity) was issued.Product liability claims are subject to insurer acceptance; DOGA GROUP’s liability is limited to the insurer’s agreement.5. CancellationsThe client may cancel only by written notice within 60 calendar days of the order’s end date (see 4.C).The subsidiary may cancel by written notice within 90 calendar days before execution. Notice is not required if the client fails to meet payment or other agreed obligations.6. LiabilityDOGA GROUP is not liable for failures or delays due to force majeure or circumstances beyond reasonable control.Only the client may take action; no liability for emerging damage or loss of profit, regardless of origin.7. Responsibilities of the ClientThe client will provide all information and documentation required to carry out the contracted services/products.The client agrees not to hire subsidiary employees during the contract and up to 6 months after termination. Breach entails compensation equal to one year’s salary of the hired worker at the time of hiring.8. Delivery. Passing of Risk. Retention of OwnershipAny agreed term is construed under the INCOTERMS in force upon signing. If none agreed, delivery is Free Carrier (FCA) at the destination specified by the subsidiary.For FCA, at the client’s request, the subsidiary will send the product to destination; risk passes when handed to the first carrier.Products remain the subsidiary’s property until fully paid.At the subsidiary’s request, the client will assist with measures to protect ownership.9. MiscellaneousIf the client’s general purchase conditions are not compatible, the client may cancel the order. Not canceling implies acceptance of these general conditions.Any differing conditions must be agreed in an agreement/supply contract. If none exists, these conditions prevail.No party may transfer this offer without the other party’s prior written consent.All drawings/technical documents submitted remain the property of the supplying party and may not be used or disclosed beyond their intended purpose without consent.Parties submit to the courts of the city where the subsidiary is registered; Spanish law applies.10. TransportationTransport costs for replacement/return of defective materials are borne by DOGA GROUP, provided they are managed by it and its suppliers.Excess transportation costs due to production line stoppages are borne by the client, provided the delivery date was duly confirmed by DOGA GROUP.11. Personal Data ProtectionIn compliance with the Spanish Personal Data Protection Law (Organic Law 15/1999), personal data provided will be incorporated into our files solely to manage the business relationship.The client may exercise rights of access, rectification, deletion, and objection by contacting dpd@doga.es.

					 Vehicle integration via CAN or LIN communication 
			
			
						
									The electronic motors used in modern wiper systems offer advanced integration with the vehicle and enable precise control of system movement. These motors provide multiple advantages in terms of performance, energy efficiency, and advanced functionality.								
					
						
				
					 Precise Speed and Wiping Angle Control 
			
			
						
									Thanks to their integrated electronics, these motors allow precise control of wiper speed and sweep angle, ensuring optimal performance under different weather conditions.								
					
						
				
					 Advanced Additional Features 
			
			
						
									Active Parking: Allows the wiper blades to retract beneath the hood when not in use, enhancing the vehicle’s aerodynamics and overall aesthetics.Motor Synchronization: Synchronizes multiple motors for smooth, coordinated operation in complex systems.Washer Pump Integration: Facilitates combined control of the wiper motor and washer pump for enhanced cleaning efficiency.								
					
						
				
					 Sweep angles greater than 180 degrees 
			
			
						
									These motors allow for wide sweep configurations, ensuring maximum windshield coverage to improve driver visibility.								
					
						
				
					 Application in various transmission systems 
			
			
						
									They are compatible with both mechanical transmission systems (where the motor drives a linkage mechanism) and direct drive systems (direct drive on the wiper arm without the need for a mechanical transmission).								
					
						
				
					 Oscillating or rotary motion control 
			
			
						
									Depending on the application, electronic motors can be configured to generate an oscillating motion (typical of windshield wipers) or a rotary motion (for applications with special cleaning systems).

DRIVE SYSTEMS				
		At DOGA, we specialize in DC motors, and our Technical Center boasts advanced technological development capabilities along with a highly qualified team of engineers. This enables us to design customized and comprehensive solutions.		
					Integrated mechatronic solutions				
		Mechatronics is a core part of our work  and we develop systems that integrate mechanics with electronics, achieving maximum control and precision. This combination allows us to offer specific, tailored solutions for each application. The result is fully integrated systems that ensure perfectly suited functionality to meet the needs.		
																														
					Transform Your Ideas into Intelligent Systems				
		Our extensive experience allows us to provide comprehensive solutions that often involve unique challenges. By leveraging mechatronics, we maximize outcomes, enhancing both functionality and efficiency.		
					
							
									Request Information
					
					DRIVE SYSTEM ON A PICK-UP COVER				
		Drive System to actuate on a pick-up (tonneau) cover. The Drive System includes compact gearmotor, electronic control unit and transmissions. Activated though our remote control the motor drives the cover to open/close. Our system is automatically calibrated, including anti-pinch and overload protection.

									Software, Tools, and Support					Vector ©		We work with widely recognized automotive software tools such as Vector©, and with standard communication protocols – CANOpen, CAN J1939, and LIN – to facilitate the integration of electronic motors into the customer's system.																													Ectis		We also offer the possibility of developing custom desktop software for integrating the electronic motor into the client’s application. This enables motor parameter configuration, access to diagnostics, and direct motor control.An example of this is our ECTIS application, which allows advanced configuration, monitoring, and control of DC and BLDC motors. It uses artificial intelligence to automatically optimize motor parameters based on performance and operating conditions, enabling faster and more precise adjustments.

DRIVE SYSTEMS		At DOGA, we have been at the forefront of manufacturing direct current (DC) motors for over 60 years. Our extensive product range is meticulously designed to meet the needs of any application requiring DC motors.								
							
									Discover Products
					
			
						
							When Performance Meets Precision		We offer motors with power outputs ranging from 2.5 to 1.500 W, available in 12, 24, 72, and up to 110 V. Our worm gear motors feature ratios from 12:1 to 81:1, while our planetary gear motors offer ratios from 4 to 308:1. Additionally, we provide electronic motors with custom-made interfaces, tailored to each client’s specific requirements.			PRODUCTS					Motor Designs that Fit You Perfectly		Our team of engineers excels in designing unique and precise excels in designing unique and precise motors that can be complemented with gearboxes, brakes, encoders, and custom-programmed electronics. This electronics can be integrated or external, always aiming to offer infinite possibilities that make each application unique and special.

Mission & Values		[mission_value_cards]			DOGA Mission		DOGA is a multi-product company and has therefore defined a separate mission for each of its product lines.

									DOGA Core Values		DOGA core values define how the company and its staff must act.					
																														
					Leadership				
									Demonstrate leadership within a team, motivating and delegating responsibilities to achieve common goals with autonomy and efficiency.								
																														
					Communication				
									The capacity to express clearly and understandably one’s own opinions and ideas. The ability to convey one’s own viewpoints precisely and intelligibly. 								
																														
					Resolution and Decision Making
				
									The capacity to express clearly and understandably one’s own opinions and ideas. The ability to convey one’s own viewpoints precisely and intelligibly. 								
																														
					Creativity / Innovation				
									Seeking imaginative solutions to problems related with work and being able to find innovative alternatives others than those normally used for resolution. Includes de capacity to bring new ideas that can turn into improvements or introduce changes.								
																														
					Teamwork				
									Actively contribute to the achievement of a common goal. Collaborate in the establishment of friendly relationships within the group and the creation of a demanding but loose working spirit.								
																														
					Negotiation				
									Be skilful when reaching satisfactory agreements that are based on a ‘win-win’ philosophy. Be able to show flexibility with new viewpoints when trying to reach an agreement.								
																														
					Adaptability / Flexibility				
									Ability to adjust effectively to different environments, people, and situations by understanding diverse perspectives and adapting to changes in work methods and technology.								
																														
					Proactivity				
									Be capable of identifying problems, obstacles or opportunities, both current and future and respond to these. This carries along with it the capacity to take on one’s own work from a broad and non-restrictive angle, by understanding that one’s role may mean undertaking projects, activities or tasks that are different from one’s job description.								
																														
					Achievement orientation				
									The capacity to steer one’s own activity toward goal achievement. It is displayed when there is a strong interest to perform professionally beyond the standard. Standards may make reference to improving one’s own past performance, exceeding others’ performance or achieving challenging or even new goals.								
																														
					Customer orientation				
									Demonstrate sensibility toward the needs or requests from existing, potential or internal customers. Focus the efforts to discover and satisfy the needs of customers in order to satisfactorily service them.

																																																											The 3 Pillars of Sustainability		In 1987, the UN Brundtland Commission defined sustainability as "meeting the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs".The Triple Bottom Line, or 3P, is a way to define a company's sustainability through three elements: economic (Profit), environmental (Planet), and social (People).																													Environment, Social, Governance		From this focus on these elements, the acronym ESG (Environment, Social, Governance) emerged. 																													SUSTAINABILITY COMMITTEE		In DOGA there is a Sustainablility Committee, a cross-functional and equitable team composed ofmembers from dierent areas: Environment, Purchasing, Human Resources, Commercial, Health andSafety, Product Engineering, Subsidiaries, and Sustainability.From this committee, we work to achieve its main objectives, and at DOGA we are developing actions to integrate the 17 SDGs into our daily operations:Implement sustainable projects and initiatives within the company, primarily those outlined in the 2024-2027 Sustainability Master Plan.Support the achievement of Sustainability-related indicators (Environmental, Social, and Governance).Participate in events promoted by the Sustainability Committee.Suggest improvements and contribute ideas in this area.
